title: grants — cache keys
description: Cache keys this module owns. Two-tier evaluation cache (L1 in-process per-worker, L2 Redis shared) drives the resolver's hot path — every permission check touches L1 first. Also tenant-scoped counters for entitlement enforcement + a warmable top-grants set.

keys.description: L1 evaluation cache. Per-worker in-process (APCu / array driver). Holds the resolved (decision, permissions[]) tuple for one (subject, resource) pair. Reads: every permission check. Writes: on resolve when the L1 slot was cold. Invalidation: per-subject flush on GrantIssued / Updated / Revoked / Expired via InvalidateGrantCacheJob.
L2 evaluation cache. Redis shared across workers. Same shape as L1 but survives worker restarts. Reads: on L1 miss. Writes: on L1 miss + resolve. Invalidation: cascades from L1 invalidation.

The COUNT of active grants for this subject. Warmed by grants.warm_grant_cache middleware; used to decide whether to walk the DB on first check (0 grants = skip resolver entirely, fall through to RBAC).
Cached count of active (non-terminal) grants for the tenant. Consumed by the grants.max_active entitlement check + the admin dashboard header.

Cached set of the top-N most-recently-accessed grants for this tenant (N = settings.grants.cache.warm_top_grants). Pre-loaded by GrantCacheWarmerHook on tenant init. Structure: sorted set (grant_id → last_access_at). Consumed by the warm-cache middleware to prioritise which grants to pre-load per-request.
Cached copy of GrantableRegistry (resource_type → { class, preflight_validator, permission_prefix, display_label }). Warmed at boot; refreshed on config-cache clear.

Cached copy of the access/rbac permissions table (name -> { id, guard_name }) for PermissionCatalogueValidator. Refreshed when access/rbac's permissions table changes (via its own cache-invalidation event).
keys.note: Un-prefixed — permissions table is platform-scoped (per-guard + per-application, but not per-tenant).

invalidationRules.trigger: GrantIssued OR GrantUpdated OR GrantRevoked OR GrantExpired
invalidationRules.action: Dispatch InvalidateGrantCacheJob with (tenant, subject, resource) keys → flushes L1 + L2 for that (subject, resource) pair AND the subject_footprint counter.
invalidationRules.reasoning: Cache invalidation is per-subject (all subject_id keys for the affected subject) rather than per-grant so downstream policy checks with different resource_ids don't return stale data.

invalidationRules.action: L1 continues serving (in-process, unaffected). L2 miss ratio spikes for the failover window; resolver falls back to DB.
invalidationRules.reasoning: L1 + L2 hierarchy is designed to degrade cleanly — losing L2 means slower checks, not incorrect checks.

invalidationRules.action: L1 is cold on every fresh worker; L2 still holds recent entries; the first checks per (subject, resource) pay the L2 read cost, not the DB cost.
invalidationRules.reasoning: L1 is not persisted (in-process). GrantCacheWarmerHook and grants.warm_grant_cache middleware together bring L1 up quickly.
